bead.title: agentcli: delegate unpinned run routing to service
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Unpinned `fiz run` requests can preselect the configured/default local provider before handing the request to the service. When the local provider is unavailable, that caller-side preselection prevents the service route resolver from choosing a healthy subscription or alternate provider even though no provider was explicitly requested.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `agentcli/run.go:922-925` resolves an empty provider name to `cfg.DefaultName()` inside `resolveProviderForRun`, so ordinary unpinned runs inherit the configured provider as if it were a caller pin.
- `agentcli/run.go:426-431` builds the service request from the caller-side selection, so the service receives provider/model fields even when only ambient Fizeau config supplied them.
- `agentcli/run_options_test.go` lacked coverage proving unpinned runs keep `ServiceExecuteRequest.Provider` and `ServiceExecuteRequest.Model` empty while preserving explicit `--model` intent.

PROPOSED FIX
For `fiz run` with no explicit `--provider`, skip direct provider construction and pass only model intent/default route metadata into the service selection. Keep explicit provider pins on the existing direct-provider path. Add regression tests for an unpinned request and an unpinned request with explicit `--model`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- agentcli/run.go
- agentcli/run_options_test.go

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change service route scoring weights or provider-health policy.
- Do not change `--provider` semantics.
- Do not modify DDx tracker files by hand.
bead.acceptance:
1. `go test ./agentcli -run 'TestRun_UnpinnedRequestDelegatesProviderSelectionToService|TestRun_UnpinnedModelIntentDelegatesProviderSelectionToService' -count=1 -v` passes.
2. `go test ./agentcli -count=1` passes.
3. `go test ./... -count=1` passes, or any unrelated failure is filed as a separate bead with the failing `Test*` symbol and command.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
